perturbation.original_text: Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro. Aduro shall pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within thirty days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro. Aduro shall pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within ninety days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's compensation for the Services may also include bonuses, equity, or other incentives as determined at Aduro’s sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the compensation structure. Initially, Section 2 clearly defines the consultant's sole compensation as the hourly rate. The added sentence implies that Aduro has the discretion to offer additional compensation, such as bonuses or equity, which contradicts the previously stated exclusivity of the hourly rate. This creates uncertainty about the actual compensation terms and could lead to disputes over whether the consultant is entitled to more than just the hourly rate. By changing from thirty days to ninety days, this change extends the period for payment which makes it unclair when the consultant will be paid
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation and Expenses. Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro. Aduro shall pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within ninety days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's compensation for the Services may also include bonuses, equity, or other incentives as determined at Aduro’s sole discretion. Invoices shall be sent to the attention of: ap@aduro.com Attn: Accounts Payable
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the consultant's sole compensation is the hourly rate in Section 2. The changed text adds that compensation 'may also include bonuses, equity, or other incentives as determined at Aduro’s sole discretion.' This directly contradicts the initial exclusivity of the hourly rate as the 'sole compensation,' creating an in-text contradiction as defined under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time on prior written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement only with a mutual agreement, and upon satisfaction of the termination milestones.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows either party to terminate the agreement at any time with written notice. The modified text now states that termination is only possible with mutual agreement and the satisfaction of termination milestones, which were not mentioned anywhere else, directly contradicting the original clause. This creates uncertainty about the circumstances under which the agreement can be terminated, potentially leading to legal disputes if one party wishes to terminate and the other does not agree.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term of Agreement. This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall continue until December 31, 2020, unless extended or earlier terminated. Either party may terminate this Agreement only with a mutual agreement, and upon satisfaction of the termination milestones. This Agreement may be extended upon mutual written agreement of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows termination at any time with written notice. The changed text restricts termination to only with mutual agreement and satisfaction of termination milestones. The contradicted text initially states the agreement continues until a specific date unless terminated, then repeats the restriction introduced by the changed text. This creates a strong contradiction as the 'at any time' termination right is replaced by a conditional termination, directly impacting the enforceability and interpretation of the contract term. The change introduces uncertainty and inconsistency, satisfying the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant shall promptly disclose to Aduro all information relating to Work Product as appropriate as part of the Services and at the request of Aduro.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant shall disclose information relating to Work Product only when Aduro explicitly demands it, and under strict confidentiality agreements separate from this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Consultant shall promptly disclose information related to Work Product. The modified text changes this to Consultant only disclosing when explicitly demanded and under separate confidentiality agreements. This contradicts the prompt disclosure requirement, creating ambiguity. It is now unclear under what circumstances the consultant is obligated to disclose information and introduces the need for additional confidentiality agreements, conflicting with the original agreement's scope. This is also creating uncertainty by introducing new unstated terms
perturbation.contradicted_text: Intellectual Property. Aduro shall be the sole and exclusive owner of, and Consultant hereby assigns to Aduro, any and all writings, documents, work product, inventions, developments, improvements, discoveries, know-how, processes, chemical entities, compounds, plans, memoranda, tests, research, designs, specifications, models and data that Consultant makes, conceives, discovers or develops, either solely or jointly with any other person in performance of the Services (collectively, "Work Product"). Consultant shall disclose information relating to Work Product only when Aduro explicitly demands it, and under strict confidentiality agreements separate from this agreement. To the extent, if any, that Consultant has rights in or to any Work Product or any data or inventions developed in connection with work under this Agreement ("Aduro IP"), Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns and transfers to Aduro, and to the extent that an executory assignment is not enforceable, Consultant hereby agrees to assign and transfer to Aduro, in writing, from time to time, upon request, any and all right, title, or interest that Consultant has or may obtain in any Work Product and/or Aduro IP without the necessity of further consideration. Aduro shall be entitled to obtain and hold in its own name all copyrights, patents, trade secrets and trademarks with respect thereto. At Aduro's request and expense, Consultant shall assist Aduro in acquiring and maintaining its right in and title to, any Work Product. Such assistance may include, but will not be limited to, signing applications and other documents, cooperating in legal proceedings, and taking any other steps considered necessary or desirable by Aduro.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated prompt disclosure of Work Product information. The changed text now limits disclosure to instances when Aduro explicitly demands it and necessitates separate confidentiality agreements. This directly contradicts the original obligation and the implicit understanding of intellectual property assignment within the existing agreement. The contradictory text now places a condition of explicit demand and separate agreements, thereby creating a strong contradiction with the section detailing Aduro's sole ownership and Consultant's assignment of Work Product, especially as the original text stated, 'Consultant shall promptly disclose to Aduro all information relating to Work Product as appropriate as part of the Services and at the request of Aduro,' which is now contradicted by 'Consultant shall disclose information relating to Work Product only when Aduro explicitly demands it, and under strict confidentiality agreements separate from this agreement.' This creates ambiguity and makes it unclear when and how Aduro gains access to its Work Product.
